title: bancalus zeylanicus
intro: Bancalus zeylanicus, also known as the Indian snakewood, is a plant species native to Sri Lanka and other parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The health benefits of Bancalus zeylanicus include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting overall well-being, as well as being used as a remedy for snake and insect bites. Herbal preparations of Bancalus zeylanicus include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be made by boiling the leaves and roots in water to release their medicinal properties.
